localSourceOfTruth: blueprint/phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md and validated 402 outputs remain authoritative where repository law is stricter.

sources.currentDateSignal: Page updated 30 March 2026; links to executive and CIO or CCIO guidance.
borrowed: Treat ambient scribing and documentation support as structured medical documentation and workflow-support capabilities.
Keep intended-use, review, and governance posture visible before implementation work opens.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not treat NHS guidance as permission for visible rollout; the local gate keeps visible posture blocked until later trust and rollout contracts exist.

sources.currentDateSignal: Accessible information-governance guidance available during the 2026 transition of NHS England web estates.
borrowed: Users must be informed when ambient scribes are used, objections must be respected, and professionals remain responsible for final record accuracy.
Routine use requires organisation-approved products, due diligence, and local policy alignment.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not open live capture or patient-facing AI features; 407 remains conservative and shadow or manually controlled.

sources.currentDateSignal: IM1 page includes AI deployment guidance and formal Request for Change language.
borrowed: AI-containing products require documentation review during pairing, including DCB0129 material, DPIA, medical device registration where applicable, and subprocessor safety material when needed.
Material evolution through AI or significant functional enhancement routes through RFC with updated SCAL and associated documentation.
IM1 does not perform AI-specific technical model assurance; local due diligence remains necessary.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not mark RFC submission ready; 405 may freeze rules, while actual submission remains blocked until release-candidate evidence exists.

sources.currentDateSignal: Page says this site stopped updating from 31 March 2026, describes February 2026 DTAC changes, and says the old form should not be used from 6 April 2026.
borrowed: DTAC spans clinical safety, data protection, technical security, interoperability, and usability or accessibility.
DTAC complements other approvals and does not replace medical-device, DCB0129, or DCB0160 responsibilities.
For current work, evidence rows must carry version and point-of-use posture rather than assuming a static checklist.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not use the retired-site status as a blocker for local design work; the gate records the transition and requires future tasks to download current forms at point of use.

sources.currentDateSignal: Current release remains the 2018 update; page notes an active review of DCB0129 and DCB0160.
borrowed: Manufacturer clinical risk management and hazard evidence must be represented before model-bearing release work proceeds.
Future evidence must be versioned because the standards are under review.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not assert DCB0129 compliance; 405 and 417 must later publish exact deltas and evidence.

sources.currentDateSignal: Current release remains the 2018 update; page notes deployment, use, maintenance, and decommissioning responsibility.
borrowed: Deployment organisations need explicit clinical-risk management responsibility for deployed health IT systems.
Phase 8 visible rollout remains blocked until deployment-side responsibilities are represented.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not allow deployment signoff to be inferred from manufacturer-side artifacts alone.

sources.currentDateSignal: Applicability guidance remains live and points to direct-care influence and medical-device integration questions.
borrowed: Digital products that influence near-real-time direct care need DCB0129 and DCB0160 consideration, and medical-device status does not remove those responsibilities when implemented in a Health IT system.
Endpoint-suggestion and clinical-decision support boundaries should fail closed.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not treat low-risk documentation assistance as outside governance; local law keeps all assistive work under safety and assurance boundaries.

sources.currentDateSignal: Official Playwright documentation current at review time.
borrowed: Use traces with screenshots and DOM snapshots for inspectable browser evidence.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not upload traces externally; local artifacts remain repository-local.

sources.currentDateSignal: Official accessibility testing page current at review time.
borrowed: Use targeted accessibility checks and avoid brittle whole-violation snapshots.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not make axe violation snapshots part of this task because the repository proof uses deterministic selectors, keyboard checks, and traceable board regions.

sources.currentDateSignal: Official ARIA snapshot page current at review time.
borrowed: Accessible structure should be testable through roles, names, and stable region markers.
rejectedOrBounded: Did not rely only on visual screenshots; the board includes data-testid and ARIA labels for deterministic checks.
